item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked it enough to +1 but I concur that it could probably be made into an even better J-lister.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Since I don't see it mentioned in the comments, I think people are missing a detail.

They found blood of 1657-A in our manly cook's kitchen. Eggs suitable for scrambling don't contain any significant amount of blood.

So either these have hemoglobin in place of albumen, which makes a MAN EGG omelet just a lovely mental image.
Or he was letting some hatch and having long pig bacon with his eggs.

I lean towards the second case. As that would explain how the hive mind learned to be afraid of people.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There are definitely problems with this article. It is a bit lacking in content, the description and addendum aren't really connected well to each other besides maybe the discovery log, and there's plenty of early-site clinical tone errors. However, somehow I'm still drawn to this in a way that makes me upvote.

Perhaps it's the pure, in-your-face nature of the advertisement, or the idea that people are cooking, scrambling, or frying eggs with deformed humanoids in them to stay MANLY, but whatever it is I can't help but smile and feel grossed-out and feel weirded out. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I wonder if Dr. Armstrong would let me make the mother of all omelettes with these?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Volgun brought me to this SCP. Kinda short, but not bad. Feels like it could use, a little more oomph! To make it stand out more.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As a bionerd, this certainly spooked me. WHAT ARE THOSE THINGS?

Happy +100!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The idea is creative and creepy, yet as others have said, appears to be lacking. I feel like this could go somewhere, lead to something, have a backstory.. then the addendum happened and I thought it was a -J, but it wasn't and I was left feeling very confused as to what to do.

-1 because it made me think it was going somewhere, but didn't and then made me think it was a -J when it wasn't.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It just seems…lacking, somehow. The idea is creepy and really interesting, but it just falls short. The idea of humans coming from eggs does not really go anywhere. The addendum was funny, but seemed almost unnecessary. It just feels like it needs more of….anything, really.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Best addendum ever, and the stuff before it is creepy enough to make it not just silliness

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No vote from me, but I can't not read that flyer in the voice of MISTER TORGUE FLEXINGTON, THE MANLIEST MOTHER-F***ER TO EVER EXPLODE EXPLOSIONS!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So, is this supposed to be the work of multiple groups of interest (as the masked name doesn't fit "Creations of Man")? If that's the case, I've gotta say it is a pretty interesting twist on the concept of GOIs. It's especially weird that this apparently ISN'T a Dr. Wondertainment creation as it really fits his style, as does the flyer.

Other than that, I'm not quite sure what to make of this. It is suitably weird, but a bit too goofy for my tastes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If they have a collective memory, does that mean you can eventually make a sentient/sapient hive mind out of these? I mean, from hatching to death by old age in less than three weeks doesn't make for a good opportunity for mental and cultural development, but they're plenty anomalous already.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's part of the horror in this. How do you know this is bein advertised truthfully?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What happens when you eat the Man Egg? It says you get manly. I want PROOF.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I know what you get!
You get demoted to D-class and terminated for violation of approved test protocol!
*ba-dum tiss*

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's a decent idea, and the -A's not being particularly viable after a certain date was pretty good, but the CONSTANT UPPERCASE OVER THE TOP RATED-M-FOR-MANLY POWER THIRST schtick started to grate almost immediately. Honestly, this being from, say, some weird New Age thing is much creepier.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think the tone of the note threw people off and made them forget what exactly was happening here; your reaction was actually what I was hoping to evoke with this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I have just read this for the first time today so I have not seen the previous drafts that may have formed some people's judgement but this kinda freaks me out. And I'm up late without sleep when everything is 200x more funny. This is just creepy and weird.

I mean I get that this is so insane that it hits a certain part of the brain, but if you take this at face value it's pretty darn scary.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: …I'm getting slightly disturbed at the number of people that seem to think this is a primarily humorous concept.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Pretty funny, but would work better as a j imo.
couldn't take it seriously as a main list

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This doesn't need to stretch the default article format or tone in order to execute its humorous aspects; as such, it is acceptable for the main list. Humor alone does not mean that an article should be a -J.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This makes a better -J than it does an actual SCP. I couldn't bring myself to down-vote it though, its too funny.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was expecting something way weirder. Like… some kind of depraved subversion of gender norms, or something. Not an anomalous Powerthirst commercial. It's not bad for what it is, but what it is isn't really for me, so I'm neutral.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Azzle, I liked it in chat and I still like it. But the fine print…dang. I had my browser up to 400% (because I'm too lazy to copy-paste, apparently) and still had to squint. I still feel like it's missing a story, but with the mention of that GoI, I think its definitely good enough for an upvote. Bravo!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The intention of the group here was most likely not to create "MANLY" things in that sense of the word; more evidence pointing to this is hinted at in that last li'l footnote. Also consider that the MAN EGG may not have necessarily been the expected, or even anything close to desired, result.

But, y'know, death of the author and all that stuff blah blah blah

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Am i the only one who feels it should be capitalized "Creations of MAN"?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: PROPERTY DAMAGE

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Come on, these puns are really half-baked and only constitute to a fruitless discussion.

item.title: Re: MAN EGG UPDATE
item.content:encoded: So now they share a hivemind? Well ok, I'm cool with that.

item.title: MAN EGG UPDATE
item.content:encoded: Well, I've had a lot less time this week than I initially thought I would, but this has been updated. At the moment, I'm not really certain how to give this more ooph, so any further commentary on specific parts would be appreciated, as well as opinions on the newly added parts.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: … Congratulations, you have created the first SCP that caused me to put on the Brawndo commercial while I was reading it. Also, bonus points for making the horrible malformed homunculus monstrosities lay more of themselves if put into a soothing environment (implying that both they were originally designed to create more of these things and that the Foundation has done this at least once).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Not sure if I say it's "forced", but it definitely should be a -J.

On a related note, I'm glad to hear Powerthirst has gone into breakfast items. Or was it Torgue?

EDIT: …No. MANN CO. Make this a -J and call them Mann Eggs

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's a welcome addition, in my mind.

I'm hungry now, though. Dammit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, i tried to avoid overlap, but the milking one was all i came up with after 10 minutes to fill that gap. Oh well. early bird catches the worm.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I have no idea, but you'll probably want to wash it down with some Mime Juice.

…WHY.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Scantron yanked the rug right out from under you on that one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was kind of waffling, since the joke certainly wasn't falling flat as a pancake, but wasn't milking the joke either. I looked back to hashbrowns out my vote, when i finally saw that juicy bit of fine print, and it started bacon me laugh. The marketing team for this company must have been a real sausage fest. Glad you could riseand shine to the challenge of writing this.

+1

p.s. this was just going to be a normal comment, until i realized i was unintentionally going to use the word "waffling" and i had to push the joke.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
One minor complaint - given the nature of the Foundation and that note, how have they NOT scrambled up a Man Egg to serve to a D-Class to see if anything anomalous happens?

Still upvoting. Managed to give me the creeps and make me laugh at the same time. Do think more would make it better, but it still stands on its own.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
These aren't your dad's puns.
These are baking puns.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1

Read the addendum in powerthirst voice. My only complaint is this: "If these eggs are kept in conditions of a certain temperature and humidity…" I think that's a little weak. It should give a temperature and humidity range, even if it's approximate, even if it's blackboxed. They are scientists after all.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I enjoyed this. You could do more, but I don't think you really have to. +1'ed

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: These remarks are really grating.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yer just egging me on, dude.

'course, I might just make this my last post in this thread. I've got bigger fish to fry.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I wonder what you get when you make scrambled man-eggs with clown milk. Other than "ohgodnowhy" of course.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's decent, but it feels like something is missing. Article is pleasantly short, though.

You get my +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Took the whole article, but I upvoted for "General human substitute".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I agree. It's a pretty creepy concept if you think about it but it wasn't executed very well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Seriously, Scantron? I mean, that really takes the cake.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, definitely add breadth to it and milk the concept for all it's worth. I like it right now, but it's a little cheesy. The concept really does have the opportunity to bear fruit, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not my favorite thing in the world, but it made me grin in a good way, so have a +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is short and makes me smile. I advocate short articles hugely. Take your +1 you blasted prodigy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I wanted to downvote, but I just couldn't after laughing so hard and reading this aloud in a shouting voice and waking the tri-county area.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Edit: I thought about it again, and I realized my articles are short, just like this. I really can't complain if cosiderable thought was put into this, so I'll tentatively upvote.

And the last addendum did get me smiling, so yeah.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: …Well played, sir. Well played.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't have to. It exists.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I double-dog-dare you to write a "clown milk" SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Seeing how the voting is going, I'll probably be taking this down later to work on it some more. So far to work on, I have:

  • Make it, well, more. Add depth and breadth to it.
  • Probably emphasize that human eggs wouldn't exactly work the same as the eggs that are normally eaten.
  • Add backstory, work on taking it more either in one direction or the other.

All additional feedback is also appreciated.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's indie.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My expectation from something called "Man Egg" would be something about on par with "Clown Milk" on wrongness. Be not confused, though, nothing, nothing will ever be more wrong, or even as wrong as CLOWN MILK. I digress, though.

The execution, I find lacking. It is a matter of expectation, I suppose, but I suppose the play on words and quasi-literarlity of the concept (as well as the usual tacked-on "do not let X happen, or then Y", except in this case Y is rather underwhelming) makes it flop.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's not Wondertainment, though. By the length of the black boxes, it might be Prometheus Labs, but I think this one is just an indie production.
